LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DOMUS DESIGN GROUP LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDICIAL REFERENCE IS GRANTED AND ITS REQUEST FOR APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 21, 2017 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO AGREE ON THE REFEREE OR, IF THEY ARE UNABLE TO DO SO, TO SUBMIT AND OBJECT TO NOMINEES. THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF THE JUDICIAL REFERENCE PROCEEDING. PER CCP 640, NO LATER THAN MAY 26 EACH SIDE MAY NOMINATE UP TO THREE PERSONS. NO LATER THAN JUNE 2 EACH SIDE MAY OBJECT TO THE NOMINEES OF THE OTHER SIDE. ANY OBJECTION MUST BE AS PRECISE AS POSSIBLE BY STATING THE EXACT GROUND(S) FOR THE OBJECTION AND ANY FACTUAL SUPPORT FOR THAT OBJECTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Request For Appointment Of A Referee
Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Request For Appointment Of A Referee is Off calendar. Neither side nominated any persons to be the referee. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Mar-21-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Judicial Reference
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SARAH A. MARSEY OF HAIGHT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-546-7500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN A. DILLICK, TEL. NO. 1-415-399-8777 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Domus Design Group LLC's motion for judicial reference is granted and its request for appointment of a referee is continued to June 21, 2017 to give the parties an opportunity to agree on the referee or, if they are unable to do so, to submit and object to nominees. The case is stayed pending the completion of the judicial reference proceeding. Per CCP 640, no later than May 26 each side may nominate up to three persons. No later than June 2 each side may object to the nominees of the other side. Any objection must be as precise as possible by stating the exact ground(s) for the objection and any factual support for that objection. Domus established the existence of a reference agreement in the Purchase Order. None of the four reasons proffered by plaintiff Build Group, Inc. opposing judicial reference warrants denying this motion. While the Purchase Order requires either judicial reference or binding arbitration for resolution of disputes, the fact that the parties can choose between these two methods does not permit Build Group to avoid both methods or deprives this court of the authority to grant a motion requesting one of them. Absent a showing of unconscionability of the provision limiting discovery, which Build Group doesn't even purport to make, Build Group's argument regarding the limited discovery lacks merit. Nor has Build Group shown that Domus waived its right to seek judicial reference. In this context, prejudice to Build Group only occurs if Domus had used the judicial process to obtain information that it could not obtain in the reference proceeding, waited till the eve of trial to make its motion, or material evidence has been lost due to Domus' delay. (O'Donoghue v. Superior Court (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 245, 264-265 (applying arbitration waiver case law to judicial reference).) Since Domus only appeared in this case a few months ago and no discovery has occurred, there is no prejudice to Build Group by Domus' failure to file this motion earlier. Nor has Build Group shown that this motion was filed in bad faith or cited any authority that, even if it was, that the bad faith filing of a motion for a judicial reference is a basis to deny the motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/hk
